mandatory_claims: OpenMobile achieves competitive results across three dynamic mobile agent benchmarks
OpenMobile's fine-tuned Qwen2.5-VL and Qwen3-VL models reach 51.7% and 64.7% on AndroidWorld
OpenMobile's performance gains stem from broad functionality coverage rather than benchmark overfitting
mandatory_sections: Why OpenMobile is a game-changer in mobile agent research
What OpenMobile does and how it works
How to apply OpenMobile in real-world scenarios
What would falsify OpenMobile's main claim
required_visuals: A flowchart illustrating OpenMobile's task synthesis pipeline and policy-switching strategy
likely_failures: Overfitting to the benchmark test sets
Insufficient data quality or quantity for training the models
preventive_checks: Verifying the overlap between synthetic instructions and benchmark test sets
Conducting transparent analyses on the performance gains
backup_remedies: Using alternative models or architectures to improve performance
Collecting more data or fine-tuning the models for better results
reviewer_focus: Evaluating the validity of OpenMobile's claims and results
Assessing the potential applications and limitations of OpenMobile
